---
title: "openbotrisk"
subtitle: "Bot and abuse prevention — demystified."
image: site/assets/openbotrisk_banner_panel_10.png
title-block-banner: site/assets/openbotrisk_banner_panel_10.png
title-block-banner-color: "#ffffff"
---

A public investigation into the bot and abuse prevention category: what techniques exist, what the evidence says, and what can be independently verified from public data.

- [Orientation](site/orientation/index.qmd) — what this is, why it exists, scope and non-scope, how to read it
- [Background and landscape](site/background/index.qmd) — threat model, actors, economics, vendor landscape
- [Technical territory](site/techniques/index.qmd) — technique families, how they work, their limits
- [Methodology investigations](site/methodology/index.qmd) — concrete experiments on public datasets
- [Public datasets](site/public_datasets/index.qmd) — dataset reference pages, framing distance, and what each source can and cannot show
- [What can and cannot be replicated](site/boundaries/index.qmd) — the honest boundary of public-data work
- [Evidence Register](site/reading/evidence-register.qmd) — structured source register for literature, vendor publications, threat intel, and reading decisions
- [Open questions and gaps](site/open-questions/index.qmd) — genuinely unmapped territory
